phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to the Hall of Mirrors
phrases.text_local: Pour aller à la Galerie des Glaces, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor ah-LAY ah lah gah-leh-REE day GLAHS, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: The Hall of Mirrors (357 mirrors facing 357 windows) is the palace's crown jewel. It's on the first floor of the main palace and is included in the basic ticket.

responses.english: The Hall of Mirrors is on the first floor, straight ahead.
responses.phonetic: lah gah-luh-REE day glahs eh toh pruh-MYAY ay-TAHZH, too drwah
responses.french: La Galerie des Glaces est au premier étage, tout droit.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the garden fountain shows
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure commencent les Grandes Eaux ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UHR koh-MAHNSS lay grahndz OH
phrases.cultural_note: The Musical Fountains (Grandes Eaux Musicales) run on weekends and Tuesdays from April to October. The gardens require a separate ticket on fountain show days.

responses.english: The fountain shows are on weekends and Tuesdays, starting at eleven.
responses.phonetic: lay grahndz oh sohn luh wee-KEND ay luh mar-DEE, ah par-TEER donz uhr
responses.french: Les Grandes Eaux sont le weekend et le mardi, à partir d'onze heures.

phrases.text_local: Comment aller au Petit Trianon et au hameau ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN ah-LAY oh puh-TEE tree-ah-NOHN ay oh ah-MOH
phrases.cultural_note: The Petit Trianon and Marie Antoinette's hamlet are a 20-minute walk from the palace. Rent a golf cart or take the petit train to save energy for exploring.

responses.english: Follow the signs to the Petit Trianon, it is a twenty-minute walk.
responses.phonetic: swee-VAY lay pah-NOH vehr luh puh-TEE tree-ah-NOHN, seh tah van mee-NOOT ah pyay
responses.french: Suivez les panneaux vers le Petit Trianon, c'est à vingt minutes à pied.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the ticket that includes everything
phrases.text_local: Quel billet donne accès à tout le domaine ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel bee-YEH dohn ahk-SEH ah too luh doh-MEN
phrases.cultural_note: The 'Passport' ticket includes the palace, gardens (on fountain days), Trianon palaces, and Marie Antoinette's estate. It's the best value for a full-day visit.

responses.english: The Passport ticket gives access to everything. It is twenty-two euros.
responses.phonetic: luh bee-YAY pahs-POR dohn ahk-SEH ah too. seh vant-duh uh-ROH
responses.french: Le billet Passeport donne accès à tout. C'est vingt-deux euros.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the gardens when fountains aren't running
phrases.cultural_note: On non-fountain days, the gardens are free to enter. It's a lovely way to enjoy Versailles without crowds. The estate covers 800 hectares — bring comfortable shoes.

responses.english: Yes, the gardens are free today.
responses.phonetic: wee, lay zhar-DAN sohn grah-TWEET oh-zhoor-DWEE
responses.french: Oui, les jardins sont gratuits aujourd'hui.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to rent a boat on the Grand Canal
phrases.text_local: Où peut-on louer une barque sur le Grand Canal ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo puh-TOHN loo-AY oon BARK soor luh grahn kah-NAL
phrases.cultural_note: Row boats are available at the Grand Canal from spring through fall. It's a relaxing way to experience the gardens from a royal perspective.

responses.english: The boat rental is at the end of the Grand Canal.
responses.phonetic: lah loh-kah-SYOHN duh bark eh toh boo doo grahn kah-NAL
responses.french: La location de barques est au bout du Grand Canal.
